problem.instruction: Read the conversation in <A> and the lesson plans in <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
<A>
(Mr. Choi, a supervising teacher, is talking with his student teacher, Ms. Han, about her lesson plan.)

SupT: Ms. Han, I checked your lesson plan and found a couple of things that may help improve it.
ST: Oh, did I miss anything?
SupT: As you know, before you get to the main listening stage, we want students to recognize the purpose of listening, right?
ST: Yeah, and it sounds quite challenging. How can I do that?
SupT: You can try activating schemata. Making connections between personal experiences and learning can facilitate students' comprehension.
ST: Oh, I see.
SupT: And I recommend intensive listening. You know, authentic conversations have a lot of contractions. So, how about playing parts of a radio show focusing on particular language features?
ST: Good idea. Thank you. Is there anything else I missed?
SupT: Hmm, why don't you also try making some creative activities? Students can sing a song or chant, or they can record their own voice.
ST: Got it. I'll try to find some that are exciting.
SupT: Great. That's all I wanted to point out.
ST: Your suggestions are extremely helpful. I'll make some changes following your advice.
SupT: If you have any questions, don't hesitate to ask.
ST: I really appreciate your advice.

Note: SupT = supervising teacher, ST = student teacher

<B>
After the conversation, Ms. Han revised her lesson plan based on Mr. Choi's suggestions. Below are the original and modified lesson plans.

Original Lesson Plan
| Stage | Teaching & Learning Activities |
|-------|--------------------------------|
| Pre-listening | T shows the aim of the listening activity. / Tasks about what will happen to a person in a picture. / T engages Ss in small talk. |
| While-listening | Tasks Ss to listen to a story. / Tasks Ss to make inferences about the main topic of the story. / Tasks Ss to retell the story. |
| Post-listening | Tasks Ss to write a summary on the story. / Tasks Ss to present on their summaries. / T provides comments on Ss' presentations. |

Modified Lesson Plan
| Stage | Teaching & Learning Activities |
|-------|--------------------------------|
| Pre-listening | T presents the purpose of the listening activity. / Tasks Ss to predict what will happen to a person in a picture. / T engages Ss in small talk. |
| While-listening | Tasks Ss to listen to a story. / Tasks Ss to guess what the main topic of the story is. / Tasks Ss to do a gap-filling activity. |
| Post-listening | Tasks Ss to summarize the story. / Tasks Ss to act out assigned scenes from the story. / T provides feedback on Ss' performances. |

Note: T = teacher, Ss = students

Identify the supervising teacher's TWO suggestions from <A> that are reflected in the modified lesson plan in <B>. Then, explain your answers, respectively, with evidence from <B>.

steps.label: Intensive listening 반영 확인
steps.explanation: <A>에서 SupT는 특정 언어 특징에 집중하는 intensive listening을 권고했다. Modified Plan의 While-listening 단계에서 original의 'retell the story'가 'gap-filling activity'로 교체되었다. Gap-filling은 특정 언어 정보(contractions 등)에 집중하는 intensive listening 활동이다.

steps.label: Creative activities 반영 확인
steps.explanation: <A>에서 SupT는 노래·챈트·녹음 등 creative activities를 권고했다. Modified Plan의 Post-listening 단계에서 original의 'present on their summaries'가 'act out assigned scenes from the story'로 교체되었다. 역할극(act out)은 참여적이고 창의적인 활동에 해당한다.

key_terms.definition_en: A listening approach in which learners focus on specific linguistic features such as sounds, contractions, or grammar in a text.
key_terms.definition_ko: 특정 언어 특징(소리, 축약형, 문법 등)에 집중하며 듣는 청취 방식.

model_answer.essay_content: Intensive listening and creative activities are reflected in the modified plan. First, in the while-listening stage, the original story-retelling task is replaced with a gap-filling activity, which focuses on particular language features as suggested. Second, in the post-listening stage, students act out assigned scenes from the story instead of presenting summaries, incorporating creative and engaging activities.

scoring_criteria.criterion: 근거: Post-listening에서 act out assigned scenes 언급

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: Intensive listening: In the while-listening stage, students complete a gap-filling activity, focusing on specific language features, as suggested. Creative activities: In the post-listening stage, students act out scenes from the story, making the lesson more engaging and interactive.

source.concept_explanation: Intensive listening은 특정 언어 항목(발음, 축약형, 어휘 등)에 의식적으로 집중하며 듣는 활동으로, gap-filling이 대표적이다. Creative activities는 학습자가 노래·챈트·역할극 등 창의적·참여적 활동을 통해 언어를 사용하도록 하는 방식이다. Activating schemata는 이미 original plan의 picture 예측 활동에 어느 정도 반영되어 있어 modified plan에서의 변화가 두드러지지 않는다.
